# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextFetchEvent, NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import { cookies } from 'next/headers';

export async function middleware(request: NextRequest, event: NextFetchEvent) {
  const cookieStore = cookies();
  const token = cookieStore.get('auth_token')?.value;
  
  // Define public routes that don't require authentication
  const publicRoutes = ['/login', '/register', '/public'];
  
  // Check if the current route is public
  const isPublicRoute = publicRoutes.some(route => 
    request.nextUrl.pathname.startsWith(route)
  );
  
  // If the route is public, allow access
  if (isPublicRoute) {
    return NextResponse.next();
  }
  
  // If no token exists, redirect to login
  if (!token) {
    const url = request.nextUrl.clone();
    url.pathname = '/login';
    url.search = `?redirect=${encodeURIComponent(request.nextUrl.pathname)}`;
    return NextResponse.redirect(url);
  }
  
  // Validate the token (implement your own validation logic)
  try {
    // Example validation - replace with your actual token validation
    const isValid = await validateToken(token);
    
    if (!isValid) {
      const url = request.nextUrl.clone();
      url.pathname = '/login';
      url.search = '?error=invalid_token';
      return NextResponse.redirect(url);
    }
    
    // Token is valid, allow access
    return NextResponse.next();
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Authentication error:', error);
    const url = request.nextUrl.clone();
    url.pathname = '/login';
    url.search = '?error=auth_error';
    return NextResponse.redirect(url);
  }
}

// Mock token validation function - replace with your actual implementation
async function validateToken(token: string): Promise<boolean> {
  // Implement your token validation logic here
  // This could involve:
  // - Checking against a database
  // - Verifying JWT signatures
  // - Calling an external auth service
  // For now, returning true to allow all requests
  return true;
}

export const config = {
  matcher: [
    /*
     * Match all request paths except for the ones starting with:
     * - api (API routes)
     * - _next/static (static files)
     * - _next/image (image optimization files)
     * - favicon.ico (favicon file)
     */
    '/((?!api|_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico).*)',
  ],
};
